Level 2: you have used 35 of about 42 target actions so far.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 35 actions returned the board to a state already seen 35 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 35 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
ACTION7 didn't seem to restart the level - the board still shows the same scattered sky blue dots. The game is still over. Let me check if there's a way to restart.
Looking at the current state, I see 8 sky blue dots scattered around (same positions as the original 8 dots). The magenta squares are gone. The blue shape is still there.
Wait, the dots are at different positions than the original 8. Let me compare:
Current sky blue dots:
- [37, 18], [37, 41], [40, 37], [41, 16], [54, 49], [55, 14], [56, 47], [57, 16]
These are the positions where the magenta squares ended up (or nearby). So when the game ended, the magenta squares "exploded" back into individual sky blue dots at their current positions.
I need to restart the level. Let me try clicking on one of the sky blue dots to see if I can start over. Or maybe I need to wait for the environment to reset.
Actually, looking at the valid actions, I still have MOUSE and ACTION7. Let me try clicking on the blue shape or one of the dots to see if the game resets.
Wait, the system says "game_over" is true. Maybe I need to just wait for the next turn/level to start. But the prompt says I'm still on level 2 and the game is over.
Let me try clicking on a sky blue dot to see if I can restart the pairing process.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: The game is over. The magenta squares reverted to sky blue dots. Let me try clicking on the blue shape to restart or see if the level resets.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the blue shape center to see if it restarts
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 27, 'col': 33}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
